title: festuca altaica subsp. arizonica
intro: Festuca altaica subsp. arizonica, also known as Arizona fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with roots and leaves being employed in herbal remedies. The health benefits associated with Festuca altaica subsp. arizonica include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the roots or leaves in hot water, and it has been used as a treatment for various ailments, including wounds and digestive issues.
